books.title: The Midnight Library
books.author: Matt Haig
books.description: A reflective story about alternate lives, regret, and second chances.
books.summary: A reflective story about alternate lives, regret, and second chances.
books.content: Nora discovers a mysterious library between life and death where every book opens into a different version of her life. As she steps into each possibility, she learns that fulfillment is less about perfection and more about choosing meaning, connection, and courage in the life she has.

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.description: Practical systems for building good habits and breaking bad ones.
books.summary: Practical systems for building good habits and breaking bad ones.
books.content: This book focuses on the power of tiny improvements. By reshaping cues, routines, and rewards, readers can design environments that support consistency. The central idea is simple: systems beat goals when it comes to long-term change.

books.title: Deep Work
books.author: Cal Newport
books.description: A guide to focused concentration in a distracted world.
books.summary: A guide to focused concentration in a distracted world.
books.content: Cal Newport argues that the ability to concentrate without distraction is a competitive advantage. The book explores rituals, boundaries, and deliberate practice that help people produce high-value work with more depth and less digital noise.

articles.title: How Daily Reading Rituals Improve Retention
articles.author: Book Stuff Editorial
articles.description: Why a repeatable reading routine helps memory and focus.
articles.summary: Why a repeatable reading routine helps memory and focus.
articles.content: Consistent reading habits create a context cue for the brain. A familiar place, time, and rhythm reduce friction and improve recall. Small rituals like note taking, short reflection, and distraction-free sessions can significantly improve comprehension over time.

articles.title: Designing a Better Digital Shelf
articles.author: Book Stuff Editorial
articles.description: What reading apps can learn from physical libraries.
articles.summary: What reading apps can learn from physical libraries.
articles.content: Good digital shelves balance discovery with calm. Readers benefit when interfaces present clear categories, room to breathe, and visible progress. The best reading experiences borrow from library design by making exploration intuitive without overwhelming the user.

articles.title: Following Authors Without Losing Focus
articles.author: Book Stuff Editorial
articles.description: A lightweight approach to tracking authors and new releases.
articles.summary: A lightweight approach to tracking authors and new releases.
articles.content: Readers often want to stay current with favorite authors, but too many alerts create noise. A better approach is a weekly digest with clear categories: new releases, essays, interviews, and recommendations. This keeps discovery intentional and manageable.
